title: AERS eval scenario
description: Schema for a single eval scenario. Files are authored as TOML in eval-harness/scenarios/<id>.toml; this JSON Schema documents the parsed shape and is enforced by eval-harness/run_evals.py validate_scenario().

id.description: Stable id; MUST equal the file stem so candidate outputs can be looked up as <id>.md.

skill.description: Repo-relative path to an existing skill directory under test, e.g. skills/00-Full-empirical-analysis-skill_StatsPAI. Absolute paths and paths escaping the repo are rejected.

category.description: Free-form grouping, e.g. causal-identification, reproducibility, citation-hygiene, runtime-safety, writing-compliance, writing-style.

prompt.description: The user request handed to an agent that has the skill loaded. This is what produces the candidate output.

context_data.description: Optional repo-relative fixture data paths the scenario depends on; each path must exist and stay inside the repo.

required.description: If true, failing this item fails the whole scenario (gating).

section.description: Optional regex (group 1 captured) to scope the check to part of the candidate, e.g. an abstract.

per_chars.description: For regex_count_max: normalize target per N characters.

extract.description: For numeric checks: regex with group 1 = the number.

guidance.description: For manual checks: instruction to the judge.
